commit.message:
Fix cell name parsing (RAD50 decoding) and add comprehensive extractor

- dgn_parser.py: Fix cell header parsing - cell names are RAD50-encoded at
  bytes 38-41 (not ASCII at 8-13). Cell origin at bytes 84-91. Now correctly
  extracts names like CLVLV, DIT1-DIT33, BOX, AROWHD, etc.
- dgn_extractor.py: New hybrid extractor combining custom parser (cell names,
  weights, styles) with GDAL/OGR (accurate real-world coordinates). Produces
  comprehensive AI-readable JSON with 10,201 elements.
- pid_classifier.py: Add DIT cell library mappings (CLVLV=Control Valve,
  DIT1=Gate Valve, DIT18=Reducer, DIT23=Spec Break, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

protocol: complete settlement mechanics — fees, disputes, timeouts, generation ledger

- Fee collection on task settlement: 0.1% deducted from worker payment, split 80/20 to validator/treasury
- Dispute resolution: auto-resolve based on verification score after timeout period
- Claim timeout: abandoned claims auto-release to open, claimer reputation penalized
- Generation ledger records verified task output as productive value backing AET
- Sybil resistance: IP-based rate limiting on agent registration (5/hour)
- SDK v0.2.0: all action methods include agent identity, published to PyPI
- E2E developer flow test against live testnet

commit.message:
fix: show Telegram Bot Token input for all users, not just those with Telegram enabled

The Telegram section was gated behind vm.channelsEnabled?.includes("telegram"),
making it invisible to new users who haven't set up Telegram yet — they couldn't
see the input to configure it in the first place (reported by Chris K on mobile).

Also fixes mobile responsiveness across all Settings page input sections:
- Input + button rows stack vertically on mobile (flex-col sm:flex-row)
- Responsive padding (p-4 sm:p-6) on glass cards
- min-w-0 on inputs to prevent overflow on narrow screens
- truncate on monospace text (bot usernames, API keys)
- Dynamic placeholder text for new vs existing Telegram users

commit.message:
perf(celery): stagger concurrent MikroTik tasks and add quota key purge + DLQ monitor

- extensions.py: add countdown stagger (20/40/55s) to unauthorized, cleanup-dhcp-arp,
  and policy-parity-guard beats to prevent simultaneous MikroTik API connections
  that caused failed:89 timeout bursts on container start
- extensions.py: add purge-stale-quota-keys (daily 03:30) and dlq-health-monitor (15 min)
  to Celery beat schedule, controlled by env QUOTA_STALE_KEY_PURGE_ENABLED and
  TASK_DLQ_ALERT_THROTTLE_MINUTES
- tasks.py: implement purge_stale_quota_keys_task — scans Redis quota:last_bytes:mac:*
  and deletes keys for MACs not seen in UserDevice within QUOTA_STALE_KEY_STALE_DAYS (30d)
- tasks.py: implement dlq_health_monitor_task — checks celery:dlq every 15 min, sends
  WhatsApp alert to superadmin with 3-item preview and configurable throttle

commit.message:
Skip uptime guard in --dry-run mode

The uptime guard prevents shutdown on fresh instances; that concern
doesn't apply to dry runs. Bypassing it lets --dry-run exercise the
full per-server idle detection logic regardless of instance age.

commit.message:
gen: batch generate all 1,680 cards — 100% room coverage (#116)

Fill all 1,361 remaining EMPTY stubs across 34 decks (02-06, 14-42)
using upgraded Order×Color-aware fallback template. First-pass content
intended for future rewrite — unblocks cross-deck audits, math
calculations, ralph loops, and exercise library coverage analysis.

Changes:
- Rewrite fallback_template() in batch-generate-deck.py to produce
  structurally correct cards per Order×Color (7 Order block sequences,
  8 Color modifiers, Order-specific load/rep/rest/intentions/closings)
- Scaffold 29 deck identities (14-42) with exercise mappings via
  deck-identity-scaffold.py (fix: 3-pass assignment with duplicate fallback)
- Update ROUNDS in batch-generate-all.py to exclude complete decks
- Generate 1,361 cards: 1,354 PASS / 45 FAIL (known edge cases)
  - Decks 03-06, 14-18, 25-42: 100% PASS (28 decks, 0 failures)
  - Decks 19-24: 30 FAIL (Performance+Circuit architectural tension)
  - Deck 02: 10 FAIL (pre-fix template), Deck 13: 5 FAIL (pre-existing)
- Add 35 per-deck validation reports in reports/batch-gen/

Progress: 320/1,680 → 1,680/1,680 (19% → 100%)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VVEJ2EjwrysWaPPKwHx4Zi

fix: read missing files from disk in track detector and keep track picker open

- track-detector: use provided file contents where available, only read
  missing files from disk instead of an all-or-nothing approach
- startLearning: add ignoreFocusOut to track selection picker so it
  stays open when the editor loses focus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add start layout setting and softmax AI move selection

- Add startLayout config: "clustered" groups each player's pieces on
  opposite sides of the ring, "spread" interleaves them (old behavior).
  Clustered is the new default — prevents instant kills on small boards.
- Replace rigid best-move selection with softmax-weighted random pick.
  Temperature scales with difficulty (level 6 temp=8 is tight, level 2
  temp=120 is loose), so the AI mostly picks the best move but will
  occasionally play a near-equal alternative for variety.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add memory maintenance system — self-correcting, self-cleaning memory

Memory now actively maintains its own accuracy through:
- Live correction: surfaces contradictions when user says something
  that conflicts with memory, overwrites with correction trail
- Contradiction detection: cross-file consistency checks catch
  when projects.md says active but Jira shows done, etc.
- Smart decay: fast decay (14d) for status, slow decay (60d) for
  structure, no decay for decisions and preferences
- Live data priority: tool data (Gmail/Slack/Jira) overrides stale
  memory entries, with user confirmation
- Self-correction signals: detects when user ignores suggestions,
  re-provides known context, or tool data mismatches
- Auditing cadence: weekly health check, monthly consistency audit,
  quarterly archive cycle
- Source tracking: every entry now records where it was learned,
  making verification and cleanup easier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Claude Code hooks integration for real-time tool safety scanning

New `sentinel hook` command works as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook,
scanning every tool call for dangerous commands, data exfiltration,
and prompt injection before execution. Also bumps all version strings
to 0.3.0 and adds hooks docs to README.

fix(k8s): clean istio-ingress-svc.yaml — remove runtime fields for idempotent apply

Remove hardcoded clusterIP/clusterIPs, status block, and Kubernetes-defaulted
fields (internalTrafficPolicy, ipFamilies, ipFamilyPolicy, sessionAffinity) so
the manifest applies cleanly across clusters without conflicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix admin login loop: send Bearer token from browser directly

Nginx routes /api/v1/admin/* to Go API, bypassing Next.js proxy
that was supposed to inject the Authorization header from httpOnly
cookie. Added non-httpOnly admin_bearer cookie and read it in
adminFetch to send Bearer header directly from browser JS.
